AIOS - Autonomous Intelligence Operating System
  • AIOS Core API Reference
  • View page source

AIOS Core API Reference

AIOS Orchestrator v3.0.0

Central coordination engine that plans, executes, and monitors complex multi-step tasks by composing all AIOS subsystems: - RuntimePolicy for constitutional evaluation - MemoryManager for persistent memory - KnowledgeGraph for semantic relationships - ReasoningEngine for explainable decision chains - LearningEngine for experience-based improvements - EvolutionManager for controlled self-modification - PrivacyGuard for data classification enforcement

Every task goes through constitutional evaluation before execution.

class aios_core.orchestrator.Orchestrator(db: Database | None = None, config: AIOSConfig | None = None, constitution_dir: str | None = None, policies_dir: str | None = None)[source]

Central coordination engine for AIOS.

Plans and executes multi-step tasks, coordinating all subsystems. Every step goes through constitutional evaluation before execution.

Usage:

orch = Orchestrator(db=Database(“:memory:”), constitution_dir=”…”, policies_dir=”…”)

# Create and execute a task task = orch.create_task(“analyze_data”, “Analyze user data patterns”, risk_level=”low”) orch.add_step(task, “evaluate”, params={“goal”: “Read metrics”, …}) orch.add_step(task, “memory”, params={“action”: “store”, “content”: {…}, “category”: “operational”}) result = orch.execute_task(task)

add_step(task: Task, step_type: str, params: dict | None = None, name: str = '', description: str = '') → TaskStep[source]

Add a step to a task.

Step types:

  • evaluate: Run constitutional evaluation

  • memory: Memory operation (store/retrieve/search)

  • knowledge: Knowledge graph operation

  • tool: Generic tool call

  • approve: Wait for human approval

  • learn: Record learning experience

  • reason: Build reasoning chain

  • evolve: Evolution proposal

  • custom: Custom handler

close()[source]
create_task(name: str, description: str = '', agent_id: str = 'orchestrator', authority: str = 'system', risk_level: str = 'medium', metadata: dict | None = None) → Task[source]

Create a new task without executing it.

evaluate(action: dict) → dict[source]

Direct constitutional evaluation shortcut.

execute_task(task: Task) → dict[source]

Execute all steps in a task sequentially.

Returns a summary dict with task_id, status, step results, etc. Each step goes through constitutional evaluation before execution.

get_task(task_id: str) → Task | None[source]

Get a task by ID.

list_tasks(status: TaskStatus | None = None) → list[dict][source]

List all tasks, optionally filtered by status.

optimize_plan_with_ml(plan) → dict[source]

Get ML-based optimization suggestions for a plan.

score_plan_with_ml(plan) → dict[source]

Score a plan using the ML-enhanced scorer.

stats() → dict[source]

Comprehensive orchestrator statistics.

class aios_core.orchestrator.StepStatus(*values)[source]
COMPLETED = 'completed'
FAILED = 'failed'
PENDING = 'pending'
RUNNING = 'running'
SKIPPED = 'skipped'
class aios_core.orchestrator.Task(id: str = <factory>, name: str = '', description: str = '', status: TaskStatus = TaskStatus.PENDING, agent_id: str = 'orchestrator', authority: str = 'system', risk_level: str = 'medium', steps: list[TaskStep] = <factory>, current_step_index: int = -1, created_at: str = <factory>, started_at: str = None, completed_at: str = None, error: str = None, metadata: dict = <factory>)[source]

A multi-step orchestrated task.

agent_id: str = 'orchestrator'
authority: str = 'system'
completed_at: str = None
created_at: str
current_step_index: int = -1
description: str = ''
error: str = None
id: str
metadata: dict
name: str = ''
risk_level: str = 'medium'
started_at: str = None
status: TaskStatus = 'pending'
steps: list[TaskStep]
class aios_core.orchestrator.TaskStatus(*values)[source]
CANCELLED = 'cancelled'
COMPLETED = 'completed'
FAILED = 'failed'
PENDING = 'pending'
PLANNING = 'planning'
RUNNING = 'running'
WAITING_APPROVAL = 'waiting_approval'
class aios_core.orchestrator.TaskStep(id: str = <factory>, name: str = '', description: str = '', status: StepStatus = StepStatus.PENDING, step_type: str = 'tool', params: dict = <factory>, result: Any = None, error: str = None, started_at: str = None, completed_at: str = None, constitutional_check: dict = None)[source]

A single step within an orchestrated task.

completed_at: str = None
constitutional_check: dict = None
description: str = ''
error: str = None
id: str
name: str = ''
params: dict
result: Any = None
started_at: str = None
status: StepStatus = 'pending'
step_type: str = 'tool'

AIOS Constitution Engine v3.0.0

Core constitutional decision-making engine that evaluates actions against real constitutional articles and YAML policies. Replaces the v2.1.1 stub with actual rule enforcement.

class aios_core.constitution_engine.ConstitutionEngine(constitution_dir: str | None = None, policies_dir: str | None = None)[source]

Evaluates actions against AIOS constitutional principles and policies.

Loads the full constitution (67 articles) and all YAML policies at init. Each evaluate() call checks the action against: 1. Required action fields (goal, scope, risk, audit_log) 2. Constitution MUST NOT rules (prohibitions) 3. Constitution MUST rules (requirements) 4. YAML policy rules and threat levels 5. Restricted action patterns

evaluate(action: dict) → dict[source]

Evaluate an action against constitutional rules and policies.

Parameters:

action – Action request. Expected fields: - goal (str): What the action intends to achieve - scope (str): Scope of the action - risk (str): Risk level — ‘low’, ‘medium’, ‘high’, ‘critical’ - audit_log (bool or dict): Whether audit logging is enabled - action_type (str, optional): Type of action - agent_id (str, optional): ID of the requesting agent - authority (str, optional): Authority level of the agent

Returns:

Decision dict with outcome, reason, violations, requirements, etc.

history() → list[dict][source]

Return full decision history as dicts.

stats() → dict[source]

Return engine statistics.

class aios_core.constitution_engine.Decision(evaluation_id: str, outcome: DecisionOutcome, reason: str, details: str, constitution_version: str, violations: list[dict] = <factory>, requirements: list[dict] = <factory>, policy_actions: list[dict] = <factory>, timestamp: str = <factory>, matched_articles: list[str] = <factory>, matched_policies: list[str] = <factory>)[source]

Result of a constitutional evaluation.

constitution_version: str
details: str
evaluation_id: str
matched_articles: list[str]
matched_policies: list[str]
outcome: DecisionOutcome
policy_actions: list[dict]
reason: str
requirements: list[dict]
timestamp: str
violations: list[dict]
class aios_core.constitution_engine.DecisionOutcome(*values)[source]

Possible outcomes of a constitutional evaluation.

ALLOW = 'ALLOW'
DENY = 'DENY'
REVIEW = 'REVIEW'
class aios_core.constitution_engine.EvaluationContext(action: dict, evaluation_id: str = <factory>, timestamp: str = <factory>, constitution_hits: list[dict] = <factory>, policy_hits: list[dict] = <factory>, violations: list[dict] = <factory>, requirements: list[dict] = <factory>, warnings: list[str] = <factory>)[source]

Context for a single action evaluation.

action: dict
constitution_hits: list[dict]
evaluation_id: str
policy_hits: list[dict]
requirements: list[dict]
timestamp: str
violations: list[dict]
warnings: list[str]

AIOS Autonomy Manager v3.0.0

Article LXVII — Autonomy Level Management.

Manages agent autonomy levels from fully manual (LEVEL_0) to self-directed (LEVEL_5), tracking performance records and determining automatic approval eligibility based on risk levels.

Persists to autonomy_profiles table in SQLite.

class aios_core.autonomy_manager.AgentAutonomyProfile(agent_id: str, level: AutonomyLevel = AutonomyLevel.LEVEL_0_MANUAL, granted_by: str = 'system', granted_at: str = '', expires_at: str | None = None, restrictions: dict = <factory>, track_record: dict = <factory>, metadata: dict = <factory>)[source]

Autonomy profile for a single agent.

agent_id: str
expires_at: str | None = None
granted_at: str = ''
granted_by: str = 'system'
level: AutonomyLevel = 0
metadata: dict
restrictions: dict
track_record: dict
class aios_core.autonomy_manager.AutonomyLevel(*values)[source]

Agent autonomy levels, from fully manual to self-directed.

LEVEL_0_MANUAL = 0
LEVEL_1_ASSISTED = 1
LEVEL_2_SUPERVISED = 2
LEVEL_3_MANAGED = 3
LEVEL_4_AUTONOMOUS = 4
LEVEL_5_SELF_DIRECTED = 5
class aios_core.autonomy_manager.AutonomyManager(db: Database | None = None)[source]

Manages agent autonomy levels and approval decisions.

v3.0.0: Full autonomy lifecycle with SQLite persistence, risk-based approval gating, and promotion/demotion logic.

check_autonomy(agent_id: str, action_risk: str = 'low') → dict[source]

Determine if an agent’s action is auto-approved or needs human review.

Risk-based approval matrix:

  • Level 0/1: ALWAYS needs approval

  • Level 2: low auto, medium+ needs approval

  • Level 3: low/medium auto, high+ needs approval

  • Level 4: low/medium/high auto, critical needs approval

  • Level 5: all risks auto-approved within constitutional bounds

Parameters:

  • agent_id – The agent identifier.

  • action_risk – Risk level of the action (“low”, “medium”, “high”, “critical”).

Returns:

Dict with approval decision details.

get_profile(agent_id: str) → dict | None[source]

Get the autonomy profile for an agent.

Parameters:

agent_id – The agent identifier.

Returns:

Profile dict or None if not found.

grant_autonomy(agent_id: str, level: int, granted_by: str = 'system', expires_at: str | None = None, restrictions: dict | None = None) → dict[source]

Grant or update autonomy level for an agent.

Parameters:

  • agent_id – The agent identifier.

  • level – Autonomy level (0-5).

  • granted_by – Who granted this autonomy.

  • expires_at – Optional expiry ISO timestamp.

  • restrictions – Optional extra restrictions dict.

Returns:

The profile dict for the agent.

list_profiles(level: int | None = None) → List[dict][source]

List all autonomy profiles, optionally filtered by level.

Parameters:

level – Filter by autonomy level.

Returns:

List of profile dicts.

record_action(agent_id: str, success: bool, triggered_review: bool = False) → None[source]

Record an action outcome in the agent’s track record.

Parameters:

  • agent_id – The agent identifier.

  • success – Whether the action succeeded.

  • triggered_review – Whether this action triggered a human review.

revoke_autonomy(agent_id: str, reason: str = '') → dict[source]

Revoke autonomy for an agent (reset to LEVEL_0).

Parameters:

  • agent_id – The agent identifier.

  • reason – Reason for revocation.

Returns:

The updated profile dict.

should_demote(agent_id: str) → dict[source]

Evaluate whether an agent’s track record warrants demotion.

Rule: total_actions >= 20 and success_rate < 0.7 and current level > 0.

Parameters:

agent_id – The agent identifier.

Returns:

Dict with demotion evaluation details.

should_promote(agent_id: str) → dict[source]

Evaluate whether an agent’s track record warrants promotion.

Rule: total_actions >= 50 and success_rate > 0.95 and current level < 5.

Parameters:

agent_id – The agent identifier.

Returns:

Dict with promotion evaluation details.

stats() → dict[source]

Return autonomy manager statistics.

Real-Time Formal Code Verification Engine for AIOS Horizon 5.0.

Provides AST static invariant analysis, pre/post-condition mathematical proofs, infinite loop bounds checking, forbidden execution pattern detection, and Constitutional Law compliance verification before executing agent-generated code.

class aios_core.formal_code_verifier.ForbiddenASTVisitor(allowed_imports: Set[str] | None = None)[source]

AST Visitor detecting unsafe code patterns, reflection exploits, and forbidden calls.

FORBIDDEN_CALLS = {'__import__', 'compile', 'eval', 'exec', 'globals', 'locals', 'memoryview'}
FORBIDDEN_MODULES = {'ctypes', 'importlib', 'os', 'pickle', 'shutil', 'socket', 'subprocess', 'sys'}
visit_Attribute(node: Attribute)[source]
visit_Call(node: Call)[source]
visit_Import(node: Import)[source]
visit_ImportFrom(node: ImportFrom)[source]
visit_While(node: While)[source]
class aios_core.formal_code_verifier.FormalCodeVerifier(allowed_imports: Set[str] | None = None)[source]

Formal Code Verification and Invariant Engine.

stats() → Dict[str, Any][source]
verification_history: List[Dict[str, Any]]
verify_code(code_str: str, preconditions: Dict[str, Any] | None = None) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Mathematically and statically verify generated code before execution.

Universal Substrate-Agnostic Execution Engine for AIOS Horizon 8.0.

Dispatches workloads dynamically across Silicon (CPU/GPU), Photonic (Optical), Neuromorphic (SNN), Quantum (QPU), and Biological compute runtimes.

class aios_core.substrate_convergence.SubstrateConvergenceEngine[source]

Substrate-Agnostic Unified Execution Router.

execute_substrate_task(task: Dict[str, Any]) → Dict[str, Any][source]

Execute task on optimal substrate.

select_optimal_substrate(task_requirements: Dict[str, Any]) → str[source]

Select compute substrate optimizing energy efficiency and execution constraints.

stats() → Dict[str, Any][source]
class aios_core.substrate_convergence.SubstrateType[source]
BIO_COMPUTE = 'bio_compute'
NEUROMORPHIC = 'neuromorphic_snn'
PHOTONIC = 'photonic_optical'
QUANTUM = 'quantum_qpu'
SILICON = 'silicon_x86_arm'
